02 Design ★Brand Book ·Strategy Summary (CP2) ·Brand Strategy ·Brand Guidelines ·Logo Brief ·Copy Deck ·Customer Profile ·Business Plan ·Marketing Plan ·Social Strategy ·Website Strategy

← Pack home
Edition 03 · 2026
03 · Deploy

Website Brief

How to read this document

This is the build-ready brief for the refresh of maxxim.ai, produced in the Deploy phase against Master Briefing v2.1, the Gate 1 decisions in the Discovery Addendum, and the 1 July refresh that folded in the 29 June product mechanics and the live Jewell partner. It turns website-strategy.html into something a designer can wireframe from and a developer can scope and build without follow-up questions. It sits alongside copy-deck.html, brand-guidelines.html, brand-strategy.html, customer-profile.html, and offer-worksheet.html. It defines every page, every content block, the two conversion forms, the technical stack, the accessibility and performance targets, the SEO and redirect plan, and a launch plan timed to the 3 July showcase.

Since the 27 June version we now know exactly what the site is built on, and this brief is written against the real stack rather than a description of it. maxxim.ai is a live Astro site hosted on Cloudflare Pages, the same delivery stack every Maxxim client site ships on. Images are served from assets.maxxim.ai. The two conversion forms are an embedded contact-form app served from apps.maxxim.ai. The site is locked (Astro) by default and can be unlocked to hand-editable HTML, which is a one-way door, so the refresh is planned and shipped as Astro before any unlock is considered. Edits and deploys run through the Maxxim engine and the dashboard at admin.maxxim.ai (Clent and Raef only). This is an edit-and-restructure of the existing component library, not a re-platform and not a blank canvas. The live pages are Home, How it works, For Partners, About, Work, Start, Contact, Privacy, and Terms. The refresh keeps that page set, re-roles it around the partner-first front door, and bakes in the four Section 16 audit fixes as explicit build tasks so the site is correct by construction.

The engine is now nameable and showable in the build, not just asserted. Three named specialists, each a process, make the method concrete: Max does the words in Claude Cowork, Sal does the pixels in the Design Studio, Pip runs the Social Studio. They surface on How it works so the page can show the engine instead of claiming it, and they always stay subordinate to the accountable human, who is the partner.

Four guardrails are absolute. The method owner is Jewell, never "Joule", in any live copy, metadata, alt text, or schema. Ownership is Clent and Raef only, and Paul Clancy is a Client Partner, never a co-founder. All partner economics stay unquoted and illustrative. Proof respects clearance: Pottsville Acupuncture is the only public Maxxim case study on /work/. The live Jewell partner site is a reference and pattern for how the seam reads, but Jewell's own named results stay under Jewell's banner and are never re-used as Maxxim's public proof; BTC, Jewell Tyres, OTR Earthmover, and Hidrive are not to be published. Two framing decisions govern the build: the partner is the primary front door, and the self-serve portal is a roadmap note, named as the north star, never a page, never linked, never built.

1. Project Overview

Project type. Refresh of an existing site. Edit and restructure on the current Astro and Cloudflare Pages stack, reusing the existing component library. Not a new build and not a re-platform. Re-platforming is explicitly out of scope; it would burn the Deploy window for no strategic gain. Because the site is locked Astro and unlocking to hand-editable HTML is a one-way door, the whole refresh ships as Astro; no unlock is triggered as part of this brief.

Website purpose. Convert trusted operators into a partner enquiry as the primary action, and give partners and direct visitors a clean way to book the $4,950 Growth Diagnostic as the universal end-client entry product. The site is a two-door conversion system that closes the trust gap with cleared proof and a showable engine.

Target launch. The Section 16 fixes and the partner-facing pages must be live before the 3 July 2026 showcase, because the showcase is the first meeting of the founding partner community and partners will check the site on a phone straight after the invite. Full refresh follows in the days after. See Section 10 for the sequence.

Business objectives the site supports (from the Website Strategy): make the partner the primary front door; drive the Growth Diagnostic as the entry product; close the trust gap with Pottsville, the gated method, and the named specialists; make the partner community a reason to join; and be correct and credible by carrying the four Section 16 fixes.

Current state and key problems the refresh solves. The live site is structurally sound but factually wrong in four ways that undermine a young brand with thin public proof. The refresh corrects all four and re-roles the navigation around the partner decision sequence.

Scope boundaries

  • In scope. Refresh of the six priority pages (Home, How it works, Work, For Partners, About, Start) and the two legals (Privacy, Terms); the four Section 16 fixes; two conversion forms served from the apps.maxxim.ai contact-form app (partner enquiry, Growth Diagnostic booking or enquiry); the SEO, redirect, schema, and analytics setup; accessibility to WCAG 2.2 AA; the performance targets.
  • Out of scope (phase 1). Re-platforming; unlocking the site to hand-editable HTML; the authenticated partner hub or dashboard (referenced on For Partners as what a partner gets, extending the existing admin.maxxim.ai dashboard, not built as a public page); a resources or insights or blog section; the newsletter and chat apps at apps.maxxim.ai (later capabilities, not phase 1); e-commerce or online payment for the Diagnostic; user accounts or login; the self-serve portal (named as the north star only, never linked, never built); the read-only client portal and the assistant Ari (a partner-choice surface for end clients, not a public marketing page); any case study other than Pottsville.

2. Sitemap with Page Specifications

The refresh keeps the existing flat structure and re-orders the primary navigation to match the primary visitor's decision sequence: understand the method, see the proof, choose a door, check who is behind it, then act. Two conversion pages (Start and Contact) carry the CTAs. Utility pages sit in the footer. No deep child pages in phase 1.

Below, the page-by-page specification. Dynamic content is minimal by design: this is a near-static Astro site, so "dynamic" here means content a non-developer should be able to edit without touching layout, plus the two form submissions handled by the apps.maxxim.ai contact-form app.

PageURLPurposeContent blocks (in order)Primary CTASecondary CTADynamic content
Home / Land the position in seconds, name the trust gap, route the two audiences to the right door. Partner leads; end-client product is the clear secondary door. 1. Hero (position plus partner-first promise; candidate line "the engine behind the partner, not the name on the door"). 2. Trust gap named and answered in one line. 3. The 80/20 in brief (Max, Sal, and Pip run the repeatable 80 percent; you own the 20 percent clients pay for). 4. Cleared proof teaser (Pottsville) linking to Work. 5. Two-doors block (For Partners and Start). 6. Community as the moat, one panel, with the live Jewell partner named as the worked example. 7. Roadmap line naming the self-serve portal as the north star. 8. Closing CTA band. Become a partner (to For Partners) Book a Growth Diagnostic (to Start) None. Static.
How it works /how-it-works/ Show the engine to a sceptical operator: the gated 3D method with Deepen, the 80/20 split, the three named specialists in the Studios, a named human signing off at every gate. Where the trust gap starts to close. 1. Hero (the proven method, run by a human, at AI speed). 2. The 3D walk-through: Discover, Design, Deploy, then Deepen, with the gate at each boundary. 3. The engine made concrete: Max does the words in Cowork, Sal does the pixels in the Design Studio, Pip runs the Social Studio, shown as a legible system not a black box. 4. The 80/20 explained plainly. 5. Human-at-every-gate accountability block, and how gating is mechanical (creative unlocks only as its briefs exist and a logo is locked). 6. What the end client receives (complete strategy and launched assets they own outright, on their own domain, unlockable to hand-editable HTML). 7. Roadmap line naming the self-serve portal. 8. CTA band. Become a partner (to For Partners) See the proof (to Work) None. Static.
Work /work/ Be the proof surface. Carry Pottsville as the first and only public case study, demonstrating the full promise: gated process, launched site, client owns it outright on the same stack. 1. Hero (proof, plainly stated). 2. Pottsville Acupuncture case study: situation, the gated 3D run, the launched owned outcome, the client's own domain. 3. Unnamed multi-year track record line (the method has run with real clients for years through Jewell). 4. Ownership guarantee restated (owned outright, hosted, unlockable to hand-editable HTML). 5. CTA band. Become a partner (to For Partners) Book a Growth Diagnostic (to Start) Pottsville case content, editable as a content block. Optional dedicated URL (see Section 7).
For Partners /for-partners/ Convert the operator. Replace bespoke framing with the two-door model. Present the community as the reason to join, not a perk. 1. Hero (license a proven method, run agency-grade growth under your own name). 2. The two doors side by side: license to deliver, or refer for an optional finder's fee. Economics in structure only, no numbers. 3. What a partner gives and gets (the membership compact), including that one named partner owns each client relationship, always. 4. Community as the moat: weekly table, cadence, shared deal flow, partner directory, annual offsite. 5. Founding cohort and the 3 July showcase as the near-term moment (run as a partner discovery and alignment session, not a demo). 6. Jewell-as-live-exemplar line (Jewell runs the whole model on the same rails as any licensed partner; its named results stay under Jewell's banner). 7. Roadmap line: the partner hub extends the existing admin.maxxim.ai dashboard. 8. CTA band. Join the founding partner cohort (to Contact, partner enquiry) See how it works (to How it works) or See the proof (to Work) Partner FAQ block (editable). FAQPage schema.
About /about/ Establish who owns and stands behind the method, accurately. Correct the ownership line and the Paul Clancy attribution. 1. Hero (the people and the standard behind Maxxim). 2. Jewell as the method owner and the quality bar, now also the first live partner running on the platform. 3. Ownership stated plainly: Clent and Raef, two owners, no board. 4. The team and roles, Paul Clancy as Client Partner / business development. 5. Accountability principle (a human stands behind every output). 6. CTA band. Become a partner (to For Partners) Contact us (to Contact) Team roles, editable as a content block.
Start /start/ Convert the end-client front door: book the $4,950 Growth Diagnostic. Built so a partner can send a client straight here to close. 1. Hero (the Growth Diagnostic, your low-risk first step). 2. What it is and what you get. 3. The credit mechanic as headline reassurance (your fee comes off your engagement). 4. Named-human-at-every-gate trust line. 5. Pottsville proof teaser linking to Work. 6. Booking or enquiry form (embedded from apps.maxxim.ai). 7. Short outcome-led FAQ. Book your Growth Diagnostic See the proof (to Work) or Talk to us (to Contact) Diagnostic enquiry form submission (apps.maxxim.ai). End-client FAQ block. FAQPage schema.
Contact /contact/ The partner enquiry destination and the general enquiry catch-all. Holds the primary conversion form. 1. Hero (talk to us). 2. Partner enquiry form (primary, embedded from apps.maxxim.ai). 3. General enquiry path. 4. What happens next, in one line. 5. Trust signals (ownership, the gates, Pottsville). Submit partner enquiry Send a general enquiry Partner enquiry form submission; general enquiry submission (apps.maxxim.ai).
Privacy /privacy/ Legal. Exists; stays. Carry FIX 2 if any "Joule" instance appears. 1. Privacy policy body. None None None. Static.
Terms /terms/ Legal. Exists; stays. Carry FIX 2 if any "Joule" instance appears. 1. Terms body. None None None. Static.

Homepage page-module map

The homepage carries the most weight and the most fixes, so its modules are mapped explicitly for the designer, top to bottom, with the mobile rule called out. On mobile the hero promise and a single primary CTA must be visible without scrolling, the two-door block stacks with For Partners first, and Pottsville surfaces early.

3. Content Specifications

Content status is marked for every item: exists means it is live and needs correction or a rewrite in place; needs creating means it must be written or produced before the page can ship. The defining constraint is proof clearance: only Pottsville is public. Copy follows copy-deck.html and the locked house style: no em dashes, no exclamation marks, Australian English, claims sized to the proof, the human always the hero. Imagery follows brand-guidelines.html Section 6: real over illustrated, candid over staged, outcome over abstract; no stock AI abstractions (no glowing brains, circuit boards, or gradient orbs). Where the engine is shown, show the structured output and the gate, not a black box. Imagery is produced with image-generate (AI) or asset-upload (client-supplied), served from assets.maxxim.ai, with Signal Blue carrying the accent rather than heavy filtering.

Per-page content and image requirements

PageContent blockWord-count targetImage / mediaStatus
HomeHero headline plus subhead15 to 40One hero image or treatment, real over abstract, 1600x900 min, optimised under 200KB, served from assets.maxxim.aiExists, rewrite copy; hero image needs creating
HomeTrust-gap line; 80/20 brief; community panel; roadmap line40 to 90 eachOptional one supporting image, 800x600Needs creating (lift from Copy Deck)
HomePottsville proof teaser25 to 50One Pottsville thumbnail, 800x600, clearedNeeds creating
How it worksHero; 3D walk-through (Discover, Design, Deploy, Deepen); the Max/Sal/Pip engine block; 80/20; human-at-gate; what the client receives; roadmap line600 to 900 totalOne simple gated-process diagram showing the gate at each boundary; one legible Max/Sal/Pip Studios schematic that shows the structured output and the gate, not a black boxExists, sharpen copy; both diagrams need creating
WorkPottsville case study (situation, gated run, launched owned outcome)350 to 6002 to 4 Pottsville images: launched site screenshot(s) plus optionally the named human or context, all cleared, 1200px wideNeeds creating (FIX 4)
WorkUnnamed multi-year track record line; ownership guarantee40 to 80 eachNoneNeeds creating
For PartnersHero; two-door block; membership compact; community section; founding cohort and 3 July; Jewell-as-live-exemplar; roadmap line750 to 1000 totalOne community or table image; real, candid; 1200pxExists, replace bespoke framing (FIX 3); two-door and community copy needs creating
For PartnersPartner FAQ (how the doors work, what is mine vs the platform's, do I keep my client, what is the founding cohort)4 to 6 Q and A, 40 to 80 eachNoneNeeds creating; economics answered in structure, never numbers
AboutHero; Jewell as method owner and first live partner; ownership line (Clent and Raef only); team and roles; accountability principle400 to 650 totalOptional team or owner imagery, real; 800pxExists, correct ownership and Paul Clancy (FIX 1, FIX 2)
StartHero; what it is and what you get; credit mechanic; trust line; Pottsville teaser400 to 600 totalOne supporting image, 800x600Exists, strengthen as the conversion centre
StartEnd-client FAQ (what the Diagnostic includes, is the fee credited, do I own the assets, who is accountable, how fast, procurement/probity support)5 to 6 Q and A, 30 to 70 eachNoneNeeds creating
ContactHero; what happens next; trust signals80 to 150 totalNoneExists, light rewrite

Cross-site content notes

  • Video embeds. None required in phase 1. If a Pottsville walkthrough video is later cleared, embed it lazy-loaded, no autoplay, with a poster image fallback.
  • Downloadable assets. None in phase 1. No brochure or PDF case study, because only Pottsville is cleared and the site itself carries it.
  • Testimonials. A single cleared, named Pottsville testimonial is the target if consent allows; otherwise an outcome statement attributed to the case rather than a named quote. No logo wall. Needs creating, subject to clearance.
  • The live Jewell partner is cited as the worked example that the partner model holds, attributed to Jewell, with Jewell's own named results left under Jewell's banner. Reference and pattern only, never Maxxim's own public proof.
  • Do not publish BTC, Jewell Tyres, OTR Earthmover, or Hidrive in any block, image, caption, or alt text.

4. Functional Requirements

Two forms carry the two conversions, both served by the contact-form app at apps.maxxim.ai (the same embedded-app pattern every Maxxim client site uses; newsletter and chat apps come later and are not in phase 1). Both are short, single-step, founder-led. No user accounts, no e-commerce, no online payment, no search, no filtering, no live chat, no calculators in phase 1. A booking or scheduling link is the only additional third-party embed.

Partner enquiry form (Contact, also reached via the primary CTA carried through the nav)

  • Fields: Name (required); Email (required); Phone (optional); The operator's business or network (required, single line); Which door interests you (required, select: License to deliver / Refer for an optional finder's fee / Not sure yet); Message (optional, free text).
  • Validation: Name non-empty; Email valid format; at least one of Email or Phone present; door selection required. Inline error messages, no full-page reload.
  • Submission destination: handled by the apps.maxxim.ai contact-form app, routing form-to-inbox to the Clent and Raef enquiry inbox, plus a lightweight CRM record if connected. Do not store enquiries in an unprotected client-side log.
  • Confirmation behaviour: on success, show an inline confirmation ("Thanks, we will be in touch to set up a partner conversation") and fire the analytics event partner_enquiry_submitted. Send a transactional confirmation email to the enquirer via Cloudflare email routing or Google Workspace.
  • Spam protection: honeypot field plus Cloudflare Turnstile (native to the stack, no third-party CAPTCHA). Rate-limit at the app.

Growth Diagnostic booking or enquiry form (Start)

  • Fields: Name (required); Email (required); Phone (optional); Organisation (required); Did a partner refer you (required, Yes/No; if Yes, a single line for the partner's name) to preserve partner ownership of the relationship; Message (optional).
  • Validation: as above; the partner-referral conditional field shows only when Yes is selected.
  • Submission destination: same apps.maxxim.ai contact-form app routing to inbox and CRM, with the partner-referral flag captured as a field so partner-mediated bookings are distinguishable from direct.
  • Confirmation behaviour: inline confirmation plus the analytics event growth_diagnostic_submitted with the partner_referred flag; transactional confirmation email that states the credit mechanic plainly (the Diagnostic fee comes off the engagement).
  • Spam protection: honeypot plus Turnstile, as above.
  • Booking link. A calendar or scheduling link (for the Diagnostic and the partner discovery conversation) is embedded or linked beside the form, not in place of it, so a visitor can self-schedule or enquire. The Diagnostic is enquiry-and-book, not online checkout; no payment is taken on the site in phase 1.

General enquiry (Contact)

  • A lightweight catch-all: Name, Email, Message. Same apps.maxxim.ai app, honeypot plus Turnstile, inline confirmation. Lower priority than the two conversion forms.

Not included in phase 1

  • No user accounts, login, registration, or password reset. The authenticated partner hub sits behind this brief, extending the existing admin.maxxim.ai dashboard as a Deploy or post-Deploy build, not a public page here.
  • No newsletter or chat app (later apps.maxxim.ai capabilities, not phase 1).
  • No e-commerce, cart, checkout, payment gateway, shipping, or tax.
  • No site search, no filtering or sorting (the site is too small to need them).
  • No maps, no live chat, no calculators.

5. Design Requirements

  • Brand guidelines reference. Brand identity is LOCKED. Apply it; do not re-open it. Signal Blue #2D5BFF as the single locked brand colour and lead, high-contrast on white and white on Signal Blue; Poppins as the typeface, using weight and hierarchy rather than ornament for emphasis; design Edition 03; Blue Frog removed, so the system is cleaner and more typographic. The recommended neutrals (Ink, White, Mist, Line, Slate) support Signal Blue; the type scale in brand-guidelines.html Section 5 is canonical (Display 56px for the hero, H2 30px for sections, Body 16px). See brand-guidelines.html for the full system.
  • Logo usage. Horizontal lockup in the top bar; icon-only monogram as the favicon at the 16px floor. On white and Mist surfaces use the primary Signal Blue mark. On Signal Blue call-to-action panels and any dark hero band, use the reversed (inverse) white wordmark at https://assets.maxxim.ai/maxxim/inverse/78512f900b9d.svg, knocked out in white; never recolour it and never place it on a light field. On busy photographic fields, place either mark on a solid Signal Blue or white holding shape.
  • Responsive breakpoints. Mobile-first. Mobile up to 640px, tablet 641 to 1024px, desktop 1025px and above. Mobile changes the hierarchy: hero promise and a single primary CTA above the fold; the two-door block stacks with For Partners first; Pottsville surfaces early; CTA bands repeat at natural scroll stops; copy is shorter and claims sharper on mobile.
  • Accessibility standards. WCAG 2.2 AA (above the 2.1 AA minimum), because it is a procurement requirement for the government and not-for-profit end clients in the universal audience, and the brand sells accessible, owned public-facing assets. High-contrast Signal Blue on white, legible Poppins hierarchy, visible focus states, semantic headings, alt text on every image (and never referencing an uncleared client). See Section 9 for testing.
  • Animation and interaction. Subtle only. Calm transitions in keeping with the Sage tone. No parallax theatre, no aggressive scroll-jacking, no exit-intent pop-ups (they clash with the calm tone and the recovery strategy is structural, not interruptive). Respect prefers-reduced-motion.
  • Design system approach. Component-based, reusing the existing Astro component library. Build the refresh from shared components (hero, content block, two-door block, proof teaser, CTA band, form, FAQ) so the four fixes and the mobile rules apply consistently across pages.

6. Technical Requirements

  • CMS / framework. Stay on the existing Astro build. This is an edit-and-restructure of the current component library, not a re-platform. The site is locked (Astro) and unlocking to hand-editable HTML is a one-way door, so the whole refresh ships as Astro and no unlock is triggered here. Content that a non-developer should edit (Pottsville case, team roles, FAQs) is structured as editable content blocks or Astro content collections so Clent and Raef can update it without touching layout. Edits and deploys are made through admin.maxxim.ai (Clent and Raef only).
  • Hosting. Cloudflare Pages, the existing host and the same stack every Maxxim client site ships on. Preview deploys for review, then promote to production. Build chain: web-build scaffolds and builds, web-deploy publishes (preview then promote), domain-setup points the custom domain. Expected traffic is low and high-value (a small partner funnel), well within Cloudflare Pages limits.
  • SSL. Cloudflare universal SSL (automatic, free) on the maxxim.ai zone. Force HTTPS; HSTS on.
  • Domain. Primary domain maxxim.ai (also maxxim.com.au), already registered and live. Subdomains in use and unchanged by this brief: admin.maxxim.ai (edit and deploy surface), assets.maxxim.ai (imagery), apps.maxxim.ai (the embedded contact-form app). The self-serve portal would later need its own route or subdomain; that is out of scope and not provisioned here.
  • CDN. Cloudflare's edge network, native to Pages. No separate CDN needed. Serve images from assets.maxxim.ai, optimised and through the edge.
  • Page-speed targets (Core Web Vitals, mobile). Astro on Cloudflare Pages starts from a fast, static-first baseline; hold it there. Largest Contentful Paint under 2.5 seconds; Cumulative Layout Shift under 0.1; Interaction to Next Paint under 200 milliseconds. A slow proof page loses the sceptical operator before the proof loads. Keep imagery real but optimised; lazy-load below-the-fold media; preload the hero.
  • Integrations (kept minimal and maintainable by a two-owner operation backed by the specialist bench).
    • Form handling: the apps.maxxim.ai contact-form app, posting to email (transactional) and a lightweight CRM. Method: embedded app plus webhook or API.
    • CRM or form-to-inbox for partner and Diagnostic enquiries, with the partner-referral flag captured. Method: API or webhook behind the app.
    • Calendar or booking link for the Diagnostic and the partner discovery conversation. Method: embed or outbound link.
    • Transactional email for enquiry confirmations, via Cloudflare email routing (free forwarding) or Google Workspace. Method: API or routing rule.
    • Analytics and a heatmap or scroll tool (see Section 9). Method: script embed or edge analytics.
    • Spam protection: Cloudflare Turnstile. Method: native widget plus server-side verification in the app.
  • Phasing. Phase 1: the six priority pages, two legals, four fixes, two forms from apps.maxxim.ai. Phase 2 (deferred, out of this brief): the authenticated partner hub or dashboard extension of admin.maxxim.ai, and a resources or insights section once cleared content exists. The self-serve portal is a roadmap item beyond phase 2, named not built.

7. SEO Requirements

  • URL structure. Keep the existing flat, clean, lowercase, hyphenated paths, which Astro's file-based routing keeps clean and static: /how-it-works/, /work/, /for-partners/, /about/, /start/, /contact/, with /privacy/ and /terms/ in the footer. No deep nesting. The Pottsville case can live at /work/pottsville-acupuncture/ if a dedicated case URL is wanted, or as a section of /work/ in phase 1; if a dedicated URL is added, it must be in the sitemap and internally linked from /work/.
  • Page title and meta description. One distinct title and description per page, written to the page's job, every title carrying the Maxxim name. Descriptions plain and claim-sized, no em dashes, no exclamation marks, never referencing an uncleared client and never the string "Joule". Targets: Home, the commercialisation-engine position; How it works, the gated method and the named specialists; Work, the Pottsville proof; For Partners, the partner program and two doors; Start, the Growth Diagnostic.
  • Schema markup. Organisation schema sitewide, with correct ownership context (Maxxim; method owner Jewell; never "Joule"). Service schema for the Growth Diagnostic and the partner offer. FAQPage schema on the For Partners and Start FAQs. If Pottsville is published with consent, CreativeWork or CaseStudy framing. No schema referencing an uncleared client or Jewell's named results.
  • Redirects from the old site (301 map). The page set is unchanged, so most paths map one-to-one and need no redirect. Action items: (1) audit the live site for any URL that changes, in particular any legacy path that contained the corrupted "joule" string, any old For Partners sub-path replaced by the two-door page, and any old Work path if Pottsville moves to a dedicated case URL; (2) 301-redirect every changed or retired URL to its new equivalent; (3) ensure trailing-slash and www-to-apex behaviour is consistent and enforced with a single canonical form; (4) generate the full redirect map from a crawl of the current site before launch and verify every entry post-launch (see Section 10). Redirects are the most commonly forgotten launch element; this is an explicit launch task, not an afterthought.
  • XML sitemap. Generate sitemap.xml at build (Astro sitemap integration), listing all public pages including the Pottsville case URL if dedicated. Submit to Google Search Console at launch.
  • Robots.txt. Allow all public pages; disallow admin.maxxim.ai and any Cloudflare Pages preview deploy host; reference the sitemap. Ensure no preview or staging URL is indexable.
  • Canonical URLs. Self-referencing canonical on every page; one canonical host form (apex vs www) enforced; the Pottsville section and any dedicated case URL must not create duplicate-content conflicts.
  • Open Graph and social sharing. Per-page OG title, description, and image; Twitter card tags; a default OG image in the locked identity (Signal Blue, Poppins), served from assets.maxxim.ai. OG copy obeys house style and never references an uncleared client or "Joule".

8. Content Management

  • Who updates content post-launch. Clent and Raef, via admin.maxxim.ai. No agency retainer for content. The build must therefore make the editable blocks (Pottsville case, team roles, FAQs, hero copy) updatable without developer involvement.
  • CMS training. One short handover session covering: editing content blocks and content collections, swapping imagery served from assets.maxxim.ai, editing the two FAQs, and triggering a preview-then-promote deploy on Cloudflare Pages. A one-page written runbook accompanies it.
  • Content workflow. Draft in a Cloudflare Pages preview deploy, review on the preview URL, promote to production. For the four fixes and any proof copy, Clent signs off before promote, because correctness and clearance are the whole point.
  • Editorial permissions. Create, edit, publish, and delete are limited to Clent and Raef only (the admin.maxxim.ai constraint). No public or partner editing in phase 1.
  • Content templates needed. A reusable case-study block (so a second cleared case can be added later without a rebuild), an FAQ block, a content-section block, and a team-role block.
  • The unlock is not a content tool. Ongoing content edits are made in Astro through admin.maxxim.ai. Unlocking to hand-editable HTML is a one-way door reserved for a deliberate handover decision, not a routine update path, and is out of scope for this brief.

9. Testing and QA

  • Browser matrix. Latest two versions of Chrome, Safari, Edge, and Firefox; Safari on iOS and Chrome on Android. The partner often first checks on a phone after a referral or the showcase, so mobile Safari and Android Chrome are first-class.
  • Device testing. A modern iPhone and a modern Android phone (mobile-first priority), one tablet, and desktop at 1280 and 1440 widths. Verify the above-the-fold hero, the stacked two-door block, and early Pottsville surfacing on mobile.
  • Load testing. Not a heavy requirement; the funnel is low-volume and high-value, and Cloudflare's edge absorbs spikes. Sanity-check that the apps.maxxim.ai form handling copes with a showcase-day burst (tens of concurrent submissions) without error.
  • Accessibility testing. Automated (axe or Lighthouse) on every page, plus manual keyboard-only navigation, focus-state, and contrast checks against WCAG 2.2 AA. Verify alt text on every image and that no alt text or caption names an uncleared client.
  • Fix-verification QA (the load-bearing check). Before sign-off, grep the entire deployed build, including metadata, alt text, OG tags, and schema, for the string "Joule": there must be zero instances (FIX 2). Confirm About reads Clent and Raef only and Paul Clancy as Client Partner, not co-founder (FIX 1). Confirm Pottsville is live on /work/ and no other named client appears, and that no Jewell named result is re-used as Maxxim proof (FIX 4). Confirm For Partners shows the two-door model with no economics figure (FIX 3).
  • User acceptance testing. Clent and Raef test the two conversion flows end to end (submit a partner enquiry and a Diagnostic enquiry through the apps.maxxim.ai forms, confirm the inbox and CRM record, confirm the confirmation email and the credit-mechanic wording, confirm the partner-referral flag is captured). Clent signs off the four fixes and the Pottsville clearance before promote.
  • Staging environment. The Cloudflare Pages preview deploy is the staging surface. It must be noindex and not publicly discoverable. Access is shared by preview URL to Clent and Raef only.

10. Launch Plan

The plan works backwards from the 3 July showcase. The four fixes and the partner-facing pages ship first; the full re

Source capture ends here — full document held in the Maxxim engine
